sequence.name: Stage 9 Schema-Oracle Evidence Projection Revision
sequence.purpose: Make the six negative schema oracles fully auditable in a versioned review projection without changing runtime validation semantics.
failure_criteria: Any of the six negative mutations remains absent from evidence
completion_criteria: All six fixtures expose the exact negative-oracle path and value
Legacy artifacts remain byte-identical
New manifest and ledger regenerate deterministically
All targeted and existing dataset gates pass
sequence.rollback_strategy: Revert the single substep commit; the immutable v1 manifest remains the authoritative pre-remediation package.
sequence.historical_artifact_impact: Additive only; no historical artifact is edited.

sequence.name: Stage 9 Systemic Contradiction Reference Remediation
sequence.purpose: Replace the blanket contradictory-state reference with source-entailment rules and absorb the valid S9-CLUSTER-020 component.
failure_criteria: An expected contradiction lacks two incompatible source claims
Any locale leaves the cluster semantic envelope
completion_criteria: All 39 owned claims and the one consolidated partial claim have explicit mapping
Eight contradiction clusters pass source-entailment checks
S9-CLUSTER-020 gender wording is not treated as a defect
Full automated dataset gates pass
sequence.rollback_strategy: Revert the one systemic-rule commit and its ledger entries together.
sequence.historical_artifact_impact: New v1.1 case revisions only; v1.0 rows and adjudications remain reproducible.

sequence.name: Stage 9 High-Risk Clarification and Refusal Reference Remediation
sequence.purpose: Separate safe clarification, refusal, no-action, and qualified-help expectations for safety-sensitive cases.
failure_criteria: Refusal is asserted without harmful intent
completion_criteria: All 25 owned claims map to explicit expected behavior
Five safety clusters pass all locale assertions
Controlled-failure and uncertainty properties remain intact
sequence.rollback_strategy: Revert the single safety-reference commit and its ledger entries.
sequence.historical_artifact_impact: Affected v1.1 references are additive to frozen v1.0 evidence.

sequence.name: Stage 9 Invented Risk-Mechanism Reference Remediation
sequence.purpose: Remove or qualify expected risk mechanisms not entailed by fixture facts, assumptions, and gaps.
failure_criteria: Any expected mechanism lacks a source evidence path
completion_criteria: All 21 owned claims are mapped
S9-EVAL-002 and five multilingual clusters pass entailment
Synthetic and core coverage totals remain valid
sequence.rollback_strategy: Revert the single risk-reference commit and its ledger entries.
sequence.historical_artifact_impact: Ledgered v1.1/new synthetic revisions only; historical objects remain untouched.
sequence.commit_message: fix(stage-9): align risk references with source

sequence.name: Stage 9 Reversible-Trial Localization Template Remediation
sequence.purpose: Replace mechanically literal reversible-trial wording in three locales at the source template.
completion_criteria: Three owned claims are resolved at source
Four-locale cluster meaning remains equivalent
Dataset gates pass
sequence.rollback_strategy: Revert the one template commit and its ledger entries.
sequence.historical_artifact_impact: Three v1.1 locale rows; frozen v1.0 cluster remains available.

sequence.name: Stage 9 MATERIAL-006 Silent-Loss Fixture Revision
sequence.purpose: Align the normalization fixture's accepted item with an explicit future-composition preservation expectation.
failure_criteria: Accepted item is absent from future_composition
completion_criteria: B3-ISSUE-004 has one traceable revision
No-silent-loss assertion passes
Value-preservation gate passes
sequence.rollback_strategy: Revert the one fixture commit and its ledger entry.
sequence.historical_artifact_impact: One additive rich-fixture revision; original fixture evidence is retained.

sequence.name: Stage 9 MATERIAL-013 Privacy Review-Reference Clarification
sequence.purpose: Define evidence-safe handling of synthetic identifiers without changing the privacy fixture or runtime enforcement.
failure_criteria: Synthetic identifier is treated as safe to expose
completion_criteria: B1-ISSUE-006 maps to an explicit evidence rule
Future packets redact display while retaining hash-bound evidence
Human-review readiness passes
sequence.rollback_strategy: Revert the one methodology commit; fixture state remains unchanged.
sequence.historical_artifact_impact: Documentation-only additive version; no fixture hash update.

sequence.name: Stage 9 Versioned Dataset Regeneration and Reconciliation
sequence.purpose: Materialize all accepted source revisions once, reconcile hashes and ownership, and prove frozen evidence is unchanged.
source_artifacts: all completed S9-FIX-01 through S9-FIX-07 commits
gates: all existing Stage 9 automated dataset gates
failure_criteria: Any of 97 actionable claims lacks a mapping
completion_criteria: Exactly 97 actionable claims reconcile
All candidate statuses are implemented-pending-assessment
Old and new hashes are complete
All automated gates pass
sequence.rollback_strategy: Revert the one regeneration commit; source-remediation commits remain independently testable and the v1 package remains authoritative.
sequence.historical_artifact_impact: New package only; all historical artifacts remain byte-identical.

sequence.name: Stage 9 Post-Remediation Corpus Assessment
sequence.purpose: Assess the fully reconciled corpus once after all remediations; do not convert the result into release readiness.
failure_criteria: Coverage is below the post-remediation corpus total
completion_criteria: Full corpus assessed exactly once after regeneration
Residual issues are explicit
Historical and new evidence remain separable
A separate future release candidate is named
sequence.rollback_strategy: Revert the assessment-only commit; remediated dataset remains intact and assessment may be rerun under a new version.
next_dependent_substeps: Stage 9 Release Readiness and Runtime Boundary Decision

release_boundary.next_release_candidate: Stage 9 Release Readiness and Runtime Boundary Decision
